**Dragon Quest Monsters: Joker 2 Professional**
**Quick Translation Patching Guide:**

1. Install python. On windows, you’ll get it from here: [https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.14.5/python-3.14.5-amd64.exe](https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.14.5/python-3.14.5-amd64.exe)
   SELECT “Add python.exe to PATH”.![][image1]
2. Download the code from the [repository](https://github.com/CerisWhite/DQMJ2Pro_Translation)[![][image2]](https://github.com/CerisWhite/DQMJ2Pro_Translation*3) or this [fork](https://github.com/saneezore07/DQMJ2Pro_Translation/blob/translation/gerbs-postgame-translation/)
3. Get a copy of ndstool or build it from source. (Note: I cannot verify the version from dslazy works properly, due to it being several years old)
   ![][image3]
4. In Pro\_Tools, rename blz\_win to blz
   ![][image4]
5. In the file bar, type cmd and then press enter to bring up a command prompt
   ![][image5]
6. In the command prompt, type python \--version and press enter, which should give you a version string like this. This verifies you have Python installed correctly.![][image6]
7. Now you’ll do mkdir Pro\_ROM and extract the data from your .nds file with this command: ndstool \-x DQMJ2P.nds \-7 Pro\_ROM\\arm7.bin \-9 Pro\_ROM\\arm9.bin \-d Pro\_ROM\\data \-y Pro\_ROM\\overlay \-t Pro\_ROM\\banner.bin \-h Pro\_ROM\\header.bin \-y7 Pro\_ROM\\y7.bin \-y9 Pro\_ROM\\y9.bin![][image7]
   Normally, I would include \-o Pro\_ROM\\logo.bin but the DSLazy version of ndstool does not properly support it.
8. Decompress the arm9.bin so the tools can use it:
   python Pro\_Tools\\arm9tool.py decompress Pro\_ROM\\arm9.bin Pro\_Tools\\Pro\_ARM9.bin![][image8]
9. Now you can run the automatic patcher
   python Pro\_Tools\\performpatch.py![][image9]
   If you used a different directory name, enter it here. After pressing enter, the script will automatically build the data and apply the necessary patches.
10. Rebuild with ndstool:
    ndstool \-c Patched.nds \-7 Pro\_ROM\\arm7.bin \-9 Pro\_ROM\\arm9.bin \-d Pro\_ROM\\data \-y Pro\_ROM\\overlay \-t Pro\_ROM\\banner.bin \-h Pro\_ROM\\header.bin \-y7 Pro\_ROM\\y7.bin \-y9 Pro\_ROM\\y9.bin
    ![][image10]
    Again, I would include \-o if this version of ndstool supported it.



And now you should have a finished “Patched.nds” file you can use in whatever emulator you prefer.
